title: AGENTS.md — planscanR.screen orientation
text: Written AI agents human contributors landing repo cold. planscanR.screen general-purpose scoring / classification / selection framework planscanR family.

title: AGENTS.md — planscanR.screen orientation
text: config-agnostic toolkit screening tibble text records topic relevance. Four layers, independently usable: Embedding relevance — multilingual cosine similarity topic phrases record’s text (R/relevance_model.R, MiniLM backend R/relevance_minilm.R). Zero-shot classification — per-record probabilities candidate labels via local HuggingFace NLI pipeline (R/classify_zeroshot.R, driver R/classify_assessments.R). Keyword lexicon — transparent multilingual substring count layer (R/score_keywords.R). Learned selection — tidymodels model learns keep/drop decision human review labels three signals (R/select_features.R, R/select_learner.R, R/train_selection.R, R/select_learning_curve.R). built planscanR family (environmental-assessment records) works tibble carrying country, title, summary (, selection model used, document_id plus scored columns). brings Python toolchain (via reticulate) leaf planscanR fetcher deliberately avoids.

title: AGENTS.md — planscanR.screen orientation
text: Three sibling repos biogain-tools/, dependency direction left→right: planscanR — outward-facing leaf: fetches records, owns cache + sidecar JSON schema. Pure-R, Python. See ../planscanR/AGENTS.md. planscanR.screen — Imports planscanR; adds reticulate (Suggests) tidymodels glue. Config-agnostic. planscanR.biogain — Imports ; holds BIOGAIN topics/labels/lexicon, ensemble select rule, review Shiny app, Yoda sync, runbook. (AGENTS.md created Phase 1 — present, defer project specifics.) Parent-level orientation: ../CLAUDE.md (../MIGRATION_PLAN.md migration progress).

title: AGENTS.md — planscanR.screen orientation
text: R/relevance_model.R — embedding_model() S3 constructor + embed_text() / model_name() / supported_languages() generics; score_records() (cosine, embeds docs per call) score_assessments() (sidecar-writing wrapper); pure helpers normalise_topics / slugify_topic / cosine_similarity_matrix; one-shot language-coverage warning ledger. R/relevance_minilm.R — embedding_model_minilm() (sentence-transformers paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2), session model cache, reset_embedding_cache(). R/classify_zeroshot.R — classifier() S3 + classify_text() / classifier_name() generics; classify_model_zeroshot() (mDeBERTa NLI pipeline, device auto-detect, GPU batching); reset_classifier_cache(). R/classify_assessments.R — classify_assessments() driver. labels REQUIRED arg (project default); persists per-batch write_sidecar = TRUE interrupted run resumable. R/score_keywords.R — score_keywords(). lexicon REQUIRED arg. Matches normalised text (diacritics stripped, German digraphs collapsed) plain substrings. R/select_features.R — selection_feature_names() / selection_features(); take REQUIRED topics + labels. feature frame fully determined records + spec (missing features → 0), keeps train/serve aligned. R/select_learner.R — selection_learner() S3 + selection_learner_logistic() (default, glm engine) + selection_learners() registry + default recipe. R/train_selection.R — train_selection_model(records, reviews, topics, labels, ...) (stores topics/labels model predict_selection() rebuilds frame), consensus_reviews(), selection_cv_metrics(), predict_selection(), save_selection_model() / load_selection_model(). Metrics --fold (k-fold CV). R/select_learning_curve.R — selection_learning_curve() / learning_curve_summary() (honest held-protocol fixed test set per repeat). R/utils_screen.R — self-contained copies three one-line planscanR leaf helpers (%||%, warn_partial, normalise_text_for_match) screen never reaches planscanR via :::. R/utils_language.R — copy country→language map coverage warning. R/zzz.R — declares Python deps .onLoad via reticulate::py_require() (sentence-transformers + transformers / torch / sentencepiece / protobuf).

title: AGENTS.md — planscanR.screen orientation
text: Sidecar /O owned planscanR. Read/write via planscanR::read_record_sidecar() / planscanR::write_record_sidecar(); resolve cache root via planscanR::cache_dir_default(). Never reimplement cache-root resolution sidecar JSON schema (currently v2, asserted read planscanR’s reader). schema change needs version bump matching reads three packages — see §4 migration plan. S3 generics owned . embedding_model / embed_text / model_name / supported_languages, classifier / classify_text / classifier_name, selection_learner defined package. Add new methods (new backend, new learner) , package owns generic — package introduces subclass. Config-agnosticism. project-specific topics / labels / lexicon defaults live . classify_assessments(labels=), score_keywords(lexicon=), selection_features(topics=, labels=) REQUIRED args; callers pass . BIOGAIN config lives planscanR.biogain (biogain_classification_labels(), biogain_keyword_lexicon(), biogain_assessment_topics()). add project default . Python deps additive. zzz.R declares package’s deps via py_require(), additive across session — sibling later declaring deps must clobber screen’s sentence-transformers. Keep declarations .onLoad, never overwrite Python environment.

title: AGENTS.md — planscanR.screen orientation
text: Required-arg discipline. function needs project config (topics / labels / lexicon), make required argument classed cli_abort pointing planscanR.biogain accessor — match existing messages. Offline tests, always. 135-passing suite never touches live reticulate / Python. Use deterministic mock embedding model (make_fake_model() tests/testthat/helper-screen.R) mock zero-shot classifier (test-classify.R). New backends get offline mock, live model call. tidymodels Suggests. parsnip / recipes / rsample / workflows optional; selection paths gate via require_tidymodels() classed planscanR_error_missing_dep. Don’t promote Imports. Per-package commits. Commit branch within repo ; change spanning siblings one commit per package dependency order.

title: AGENTS.md — planscanR.screen orientation
text: Generics-ownership table sidecar-schema contract: §4 parent MIGRATION_PLAN.md. Cosine scoring entry point: score_records() / score_assessments(write_sidecar = TRUE). Selection model round-trip: train_selection_model() → save_selection_model() (conventionally selection_model.rds cache root) → load_selection_model() → predict_selection(). BIOGAIN-specific wiring (ensemble rule, runbook, review app): planscanR.biogain repo, .

title: Scoring records by topic relevance
text: Pass topic record’s title summary compared multilingual embedding model, producing relevance score -1 1 (higher means closer match). topic just short phrase; pass one, several named ones: adds relevance_score_wind relevance_score_solar columns. comparison uses multilingual model, English topic phrase still matches Dutch German text — need translate topics per country. embedding model pluggable. built-backend embedding_model_minilm() (sentence-transformers MiniLM, via reticulate); wrap embedding function embedding_model() use different one. One-time setup. MiniLM backend runs small Python model reticulate; package declares sentence-transformers via reticulate::py_require() load, provisioned first use.
code: records <- planscanR::index_cache(country = "nl") records <- score_assessments(   records,   topic = c(wind = "wind energy", solar = "solar energy") )

title: Scoring records by topic relevance
text: Two complementary signals layer top cosine score. take vocabulary explicit argument — framework ships project defaults (BIOGAIN sets live planscanR.biogain):
code: # Zero-shot classification against a labelled set of candidate classes. records <- classify_assessments(   records,   labels = planscanR.biogain::biogain_classification_labels() )  # A transparent lexical (substring) count over a multilingual lexicon. records <- score_keywords(   records,   lexicon = planscanR.biogain::biogain_keyword_lexicon() )

title: Scoring records by topic relevance
text: Given human keep/drop labels (e.g. BIOGAIN review app), can train selection model per-record scores instead hand-tuning rule: model stores topics/labels predict_selection() rebuilds exactly feature frame trained (train/serve skew).
code: model <- train_selection_model(   records,   reviews,   topics = planscanR.biogain::biogain_assessment_topics(),   labels = planscanR.biogain::biogain_classification_labels() ) records <- predict_selection(model, records)

title: Scoring records by topic relevance
text: ?score_assessments, ?score_records — relevance scoring options. ?classify_assessments, ?classify_model_zeroshot — classifier. ?train_selection_model, ?selection_features — learned selection model. planscanR.biogain — BIOGAIN topic/label/keyword config select_assessments() ensemble rule combines signals.

what: Classify assessment records with a zero-shot model. — classify_assessments
title: Classify assessment records with a zero-shot model. — classify_assessments
text: Offline pass (portal calls): record, classifies title + summary + category labels adds verdict new columns. Pairs planscanR::index_cache() score_assessments() — harvest-broad-classify-later workflow.

title: Classify assessment records with a zero-shot model. — classify_assessments
code: classify_assessments(   records,   classifier = NULL,   labels,   multi_label = FALSE,   batch_size = 64L,   write_sidecar = FALSE )

title: Classify assessment records with a zero-shot model. — classify_assessments
text: records tibble (planscanR::get_assessments() / planscanR::index_cache()); must least title (ideally summary, native_type). classifier planscanR_classifier. Defaults classify_model_zeroshot(). labels Named character vector candidate labels (required). Names stable slugs used column suffixes (class_score_<slug>); values natural-language hypotheses fed zero-shot model. relevant attribute marks slugs count relevant; absent, every label treated relevant. BIOGAIN set biogain_classification_labels() planscanR.biogain package. multi_label FALSE (default) labels mutually exclusive (softmax); negative classes compete positive ones, point. TRUE scores label independently. batch_size Number records handed classifier per call — controls progress granularity R-Python round trips. distinct model's GPU batch_size (set classify_model_zeroshot()), controls NLI pairs batched device. write_sidecar TRUE, persist verdict record's sidecar JSON. Default FALSE (-memory ).

title: Classify assessment records with a zero-shot model. — classify_assessments
text: records class_* columns added.

title: Classify assessment records with a zero-shot model. — classify_assessments
text: Added columns: class_label — best label slug. class_score — probability best label. class_relevant — TRUE best label relevant attribute labels (.e. energy class, negative class). class_model — classifier's name. class_score_<slug> — one column per label probability.

title: Classify assessment records with a zero-shot model. — classify_assessments
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ recs <- index_cache(country = "de") classified <- classify_assessments(recs, write_sidecar = TRUE) table(classified$class_label, classified$class_relevant) } # }

what: Local zero-shot classifier backed by a HuggingFace NLI model. — classify_model_zeroshot
title: Local zero-shot classifier backed by a HuggingFace NLI model. — classify_model_zeroshot
text: Lazily constructs transformers zero-shot-classification pipeline first use (cached session). default model multilingual NLI model MoritzLaurer/mDeBERTa-v3-base-xnli-multilingual-nli-2mil7, handles German / Dutch / English text natively — translation required.

title: Local zero-shot classifier backed by a HuggingFace NLI model. — classify_model_zeroshot
Local zero-shot classifier backed by a HuggingFace NLI model. — classify_model_zeroshot
text: model_id HuggingFace model ID. device Torch device: "mps", "cuda", "cpu", NULL auto-detect (MPS Apple Silicon, else CUDA, else CPU). batch_size GPU batch size pipeline (number text-hypothesis NLI pairs evaluated per forward pass). single GPU main throughput lever: 16 roughly doubles throughput unbatched MPS. Larger values (e.g. 64) often slower variable-length sequences get padded batch maximum, long outliers dominate — 16 good default. benefit process-level parallelism single GPU (workers contend one device reloads model).

title: Local zero-shot classifier backed by a HuggingFace NLI model. — classify_model_zeroshot
text: planscanR_classifier_zeroshot S3 object.

title: Local zero-shot classifier backed by a HuggingFace NLI model. — classify_model_zeroshot
text: Requires Python packages transformers, torch, sentencepiece protobuf (last two needed mDeBERTa tokenizer). Install reticulate::py_require(c("transformers","torch","sentencepiece","protobuf")).

title: Local zero-shot classifier backed by a HuggingFace NLI model. — classify_model_zeroshot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clf <- classify_model_zeroshot() labels <- c(wind = "wind energy project", other = "unrelated to energy") classify_text(clf, c("Windpark Test", "Wohnungsbau"), labels) } # }

what: Classify a character vector against candidate labels. — classify_text
title: Classify a character vector against candidate labels. — classify_text
text: Returns numeric matrix one row per element x one column per label (columns named label slugs, .e. names(labels)). Values label probabilities. multi_label = FALSE row sums ~1 (softmax labels); multi_label = TRUE cell independent probability.

title: Classify a character vector against candidate labels. — classify_text
Classify a character vector against candidate labels. — classify_text
text: model planscanR_classifier object. x Character vector classify. labels Named character vector: names stable slugs (used output column names), values natural-language hypothesis phrases fed model. multi_label Logical; passed backend.

title: Classify a character vector against candidate labels. — classify_text
text: Numeric matrix [length(x), length(labels)].

what: Resolve a review store to one agreed decision per record. — consensus_reviews
title: Resolve a review store to one agreed decision per record. — consensus_reviews
text: Collapses per-(record, reviewer) review rows single ground-truth decision per (country, document_id), two steps:

title: Resolve a review store to one agreed decision per record. — consensus_reviews
Resolve a review store to one agreed decision per record. — consensus_reviews
text: reviews review-decision tibble (e.g. app's reviews.csv), document_id, country, decision, reviewer, reviewed_at. decisions Decisions treated verdict. Default c("keep", "drop") ("unsure" rows ignored).

title: Resolve a review store to one agreed decision per record. — consensus_reviews
text: tibble one row per agreed record: document_id, country, decision, n_reviewers, reviewed_at (latest among agreeing reviewers). Empty record agreed decision.

title: Resolve a review store to one agreed decision per record. — consensus_reviews
text: Per reviewer: keep reviewer's -recent verdict record (re-deciding overrides earlier opinion). Across reviewers: keep record reviewers unanimously agree. Records conflicting decisions dropped ambiguous ground truth. Records reviewed single reviewer trivially pass. rule used training labels (train_selection_model()) automated-vs-human comparison, disagreement never silently becomes label.

title: Resolve a review store to one agreed decision per record. — consensus_reviews

title: Build a custom planscanR embedding model. — embedding_model
Build a custom planscanR embedding model. — embedding_model
text: name Character scalar. Used language-support warning sidecar JSON record model produced score. languages Character vector ISO-639-1 codes model documented support. Used score_records() warn record's country language falls outside set. embed_fn function one argument (x, character vector) returns numeric matrix one row per element stable embedding dimension across calls.

title: Build a custom planscanR embedding model. — embedding_model

title: Build a custom planscanR embedding model. — embedding_model
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Trivial example: hash-based "embeddings" for testing em <- embedding_model(   name = "fake-hash",   languages = c("en", "nl"),   embed_fn = function(x) {     matrix(rnorm(length(x) * 32), nrow = length(x))   } ) supported_languages(em) embed_text(em, c("hello", "hallo")) } # }

what: Build an embedding model backed by sentence-transformers. — embedding_model_minilm
title: Build an embedding model backed by sentence-transformers. — embedding_model_minilm
text: First call lazily initialises Python SentenceTransformer via reticulate::import(). model cached session-level environment; subsequent calls re-use . Python package sentence-transformers must available — see reticulate::py_install().

title: Build an embedding model backed by sentence-transformers. — embedding_model_minilm
Build an embedding model backed by sentence-transformers. — embedding_model_minilm

what: planscanR.screen: score, classify, and select text records by topic relevance — planscanR.screen-package
title: planscanR.screen: score, classify, and select text records by topic relevance — planscanR.screen-package
text: general-purpose screening framework planscanR family:

title: planscanR.screen: score, classify, and select text records by topic relevance — planscanR.screen-package
text: Relevance scoring — score_assessments() / score_records() pluggable embedding_model() (built-MiniLM backend embedding_model_minilm()). Zero-shot classification — classify_assessments() pluggable classifier() (built-classify_model_zeroshot()). Lexical keywords — score_keywords() caller-supplied lexicon. Learned selection — train_selection_model() / predict_selection() per-record scores, pluggable selection_learner(). Reads writes planscanR sidecar cache planscanR:: (cache owner). BIOGAIN-specific topics, labels, lexicon, ensemble rule live planscanR.biogain.

title: planscanR.screen: score, classify, and select text records by topic relevance — planscanR.screen-package
text: Maintainer: Bart Hoekstra mail@barthoekstra.nl

what: Predict the learned selection decision for records. — predict_selection
title: Predict the learned selection decision for records. — predict_selection

Predict the learned selection decision for records. — predict_selection

what: Re-score an existing planscanR result tibble against (additional) topics. — score_assessments
title: Re-score an existing planscanR result tibble against (additional) topics. — score_assessments
text: Thin wrapper score_records() tuned case already tibble records (planscanR::get_assessments() planscanR::index_cache()) want add — refresh — relevance scores without re-fetching portal data. Optionally writes updated scores back -disk sidecars planscanR::index_cache() keeps visible next session.

title: Re-score an existing planscanR result tibble against (additional) topics. — score_assessments
code: score_assessments(records, topic, model = NULL, write_sidecar = FALSE)

title: Re-score an existing planscanR result tibble against (additional) topics. — score_assessments
text: records tibble planscanR result shape. topic Single string named character vector. See score_records(). model planscanR_embedding_model. Defaults embedding_model_minilm(). write_sidecar TRUE, every scored record's sidecar JSON re-written merged scores. Default FALSE (-memory ).

title: Re-score an existing planscanR result tibble against (additional) topics. — score_assessments
text: input tibble additional relevance_score_* columns (relevance_model).

title: Re-score an existing planscanR result tibble against (additional) topics. — score_assessments
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Reload everything in the cache and score against new topics offline. recs <- index_cache("/path/to/cache") scored <- score_assessments(   recs,   c(wind  = "wind energy",     solar = "solar energy",     res   = "regional energy transition strategy and planning"),   write_sidecar = TRUE ) } # }

what: Score records against the keyword lexicon. — score_keywords
title: Score records against the keyword lexicon. — score_keywords
text: Adds one kw_<topic> integer column per lexicon topic (number term occurrences record's title + summary + category) plus kw_total column. Matching normalised text (lowercased, diacritics stripped, German vowel digraphs collapsed). term matched plain substring, stem like "wind" also matches compounds "windpark".

title: Score records against the keyword lexicon. — score_keywords
Score records against the keyword lexicon. — score_keywords
text: records tibble; uses title, summary, (present) native_type. lexicon Named list term vectors, one per topic (required). BIOGAIN lexicon biogain_keyword_lexicon() planscanR.biogain package. text_fn Optional function(record) -> character building text scan. Default concatenates title + summary + native_type.

title: Score records against the keyword lexicon. — score_keywords
text: records kw_<topic> kw_total columns added.

title: Score records against the keyword lexicon. — score_keywords
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ recs <- index_cache(country = "nl") scored <- score_keywords(recs, lexicon = biogain_keyword_lexicon()) scored[scored$kw_total == 0, "title"] # likely non-energy } # }

what: Compute relevance scores for a set of records against one or more topics. — score_records
title: Compute relevance scores for a set of records against one or more topics. — score_records
text: Cosine similarity topic's embedding record's title + summary embedding. Records embedded per call regardless many topics passed, adding extra topics essentially free.

title: Compute relevance scores for a set of records against one or more topics. — score_records
Compute relevance scores for a set of records against one or more topics. — score_records
text: records tibble; must include least country, title, summary. topic character vector topic phrases. Pass named vector control column-suffix slugs; unnamed elements get auto-slugified phrase. See examples. model planscanR_embedding_model object. text_fn Optional function function(record) -> character builds text embed record. Default concatenates title summary.

title: Compute relevance scores for a set of records against one or more topics. — score_records
text: input records tibble one relevance_score_<slug> column per topic plus single relevance_model column.

title: Compute relevance scores for a set of records against one or more topics. — score_records
text: Emits one-shot warning record's country language falls outside supported_languages(model).

title: Compute relevance scores for a set of records against one or more topics. — score_records
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single topic — column will be relevance_score_wind_energy score_records(recs, "wind energy", em)  # Multiple topics in one pass — column names will be relevance_score_wind etc. score_records(   recs,   c(wind  = "wind energy",     solar = "solar energy",     res   = "regional energy transition strategy and planning"),   em ) } # }

what: Out-of-fold metrics for a trained model at an arbitrary threshold. — selection_cv_metrics
title: Out-of-fold metrics for a trained model at an arbitrary threshold. — selection_cv_metrics
text: Recomputes precision/recall/F1 + confusion model's stored --fold predictions — retraining — UI can sweep decision threshold cheaply.

title: Out-of-fold metrics for a trained model at an arbitrary threshold. — selection_cv_metrics
Out-of-fold metrics for a trained model at an arbitrary threshold. — selection_cv_metrics
text: model planscanR_selection_model. threshold Probability cutoff (defaults model's ). by_country TRUE, return one row per country plus "" row (country column prepended); otherwise single overall row.

title: Out-of-fold metrics for a trained model at an arbitrary threshold. — selection_cv_metrics
text: metrics tibble (one row, one per country + "" by_country = TRUE), NULL model OOF data.

what: Names of the features the selection model is trained on. — selection_feature_names
title: Names of the features the selection model is trained on. — selection_feature_names
text: default set three numeric relevance signals already persisted every sidecar: one cosine score per BIOGAIN topic (relevance_score_<slug>), one zero-shot classifier score per candidate label (class_score_<slug>), keyword total (kw_total).

title: Names of the features the selection model is trained on. — selection_feature_names
Names of the features the selection model is trained on. — selection_feature_names
text: topics Named topic vector naming cosine columns (required). BIOGAIN set biogain_assessment_topics() planscanR.biogain. labels Named classifier-label vector naming classifier columns (required). BIOGAIN set biogain_classification_labels() planscanR.biogain. include Optional extra feature columns append (default). Recognised: "country", "native_type". country-specific transfer unseen portal — opt training predicting set countries.

title: Names of the features the selection model is trained on. — selection_feature_names
text: character vector feature column names, stable order.

title: Names of the features the selection model is trained on. — selection_feature_names
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ selection_feature_names(topics, labels) selection_feature_names(topics, labels, include = "country") } # }

what: Build the selection-model feature frame from records. — selection_features
title: Build the selection-model feature frame from records. — selection_features
text: Produces tibble carrying record keys (document_id, country) plus one column per feature selection_feature_names(). Missing non-finite numeric features filled 0 (unscored / unclassified record reads "signal"), frame fully determined records feature spec — key property keeps training prediction aligned.

title: Build the selection-model feature frame from records. — selection_features
Build the selection-model feature frame from records. — selection_features
text: records tibble document_id, country, relevance_score_* / class_score_* / kw_total columns (whatever present; absent columns treated 0). topics Named topic vector naming cosine columns (required). BIOGAIN set biogain_assessment_topics() planscanR.biogain. labels Named classifier-label vector naming classifier columns (required). BIOGAIN set biogain_classification_labels() planscanR.biogain. include Optional extra feature columns append (default). Recognised: "country", "native_type". country-specific transfer unseen portal — opt training predicting set countries.

title: Build the selection-model feature frame from records. — selection_features
text: tibble document_id, country, feature columns, "feature_names" attribute.

title: Build the selection-model feature frame from records. — selection_features
text: returned tibble carries "feature_names" attribute listing predictor columns (everything except two key columns), trainer uses assign recipe roles.

title: Build the selection-model feature frame from records. — selection_features
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ recs <- index_cache(country = "nl") X <- selection_features(recs) attr(X, "feature_names") } # }

what: Construct a custom selection learner. — selection_learner
title: Construct a custom selection learner. — selection_learner
text: Wraps parsnip model specification S3 object trainer understands. Use built-constructors (selection_learner_logistic() friends) common cases, generic constructor plug parsnip classification spec.

title: Construct a custom selection learner. — selection_learner
code: selection_learner(name, spec, engine_pkg = character(0), recipe_fn = NULL)

title: Construct a custom selection learner. — selection_learner
text: name Character scalar; stable identifier stored fitted model shown app. spec parsnip model specification classification mode (e.g. parsnip::logistic_reg()), — preferably — zero-argument function returns one. function form keeps construction lazy, learner can created (listed UI) without parsnip installed; spec built fit time, behind train_selection_model() dependency check. engine_pkg Character vector package names engine needs fit time (besides tidymodels glue). Checked train_selection_model(). recipe_fn Optional function(train, feature_names) -> recipe overriding default preprocessing. NULL uses package default (impute-via-feature-contract, dummy-encode nominals, drop zero-variance, normalise numerics).

title: Construct a custom selection learner. — selection_learner
text: S3 object class planscanR_selection_learner.

title: Construct a custom selection learner. — selection_learner

title: Registry of the built-in learners. — selection_learners
Registry of the built-in learners. — selection_learners
text: available_only TRUE, return learners can actually trained (tidymodels packages installed).

Built-in selection learner. — selection_learners_builtin
text: Logistic regression base-R glm engine — default BIOGAIN selection model trains. Needs tidymodels glue (parsnip / recipes / rsample / workflows), extra modelling backend.

Built-in selection learner. — selection_learners_builtin
text: planscanR_selection_learner.

title: Built-in selection learner. — selection_learners_builtin

what: Learning curve for the learned selection model. — selection_learning_curve
title: Learning curve for the learned selection model. — selection_learning_curve
text: Estimates model's held-F1 / precision / recall improve number human keep/drop labels grows. repeat makes one stratified train/test split (test set FIXED across training sizes within repeat, metric comparable training pool grows), fits learner increasing stratified subsamples training pool scores held-test set. Repeating whole thing repeats times averaging (see learning_curve_summary()) smooths split noise shows curve flattens.

title: Learning curve for the learned selection model. — selection_learning_curve
code: selection_learning_curve(   records,   reviews,   topics,   labels,   learner = selection_learner_logistic(),   sizes = NULL,   test_frac = 0.25,   repeats = 10,   eval_source = "random",   threshold = 0.5,   seed = NULL,   by_country = FALSE )

title: Learning curve for the learned selection model. — selection_learning_curve
text: records scored + classified tibble (planscanR::get_assessments(), planscanR::index_cache(), review-app snapshot) carrying selection_features() columns. reviews review-decision tibble (app's reviews.csv), document_id, country, decision, source, reviewed_at. topics, labels topic classifier-label vectors naming feature columns (required); see selection_feature_names(). learner selection_learner. Defaults selection_learner_logistic(). sizes Optional integer vector training-label counts evaluate. NULL builds grid ~10-12 increasing sizes 30 maximum train-pool size (always including maximum). Supplied sizes larger train pool dropped, pool maximum always kept. test_frac Fraction labelled data held fixed test set (per repeat). repeats Number repeated held-resamples. eval_source Restrict labels review source (default "random" — unbiased sample). NULL uses every keep/drop label. threshold Probability cutoff keep decision scoring. seed Optional RNG seed reproducible splits. by_country TRUE, also slice held-test set per country compute one set metrics per country every (size, repeat), plus "" row matches default output. country column prepended. model still trained corpus-wide — varies evaluation slice, shows shared model performs training pool grows. Slices smaller 1 record dropped silently.

title: Learning curve for the learned selection model. — selection_learning_curve
text: long tibble one row per (size, repeat), columns order: size, n_train_used, rep, n_test, precision, recall, f1. by_country = TRUE, leading country column added one row per (country, size, repeat) plus "" row per (size, repeat).

title: Learning curve for the learned selection model. — selection_learning_curve
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ recs <- index_cache(country = "nl") rev <- read.csv(file.path(cache_dir, "reviews.csv"), colClasses = "character") curve <- selection_learning_curve(recs, rev, repeats = 10) learning_curve_summary(curve) } # }

title: Report which ISO-639-1 languages a model has been trained on. — supported_languages
text: Used score_records() warn (one-shot per language/model) record's country language falls outside model's supported set.

title: Report which ISO-639-1 languages a model has been trained on. — supported_languages
Report which ISO-639-1 languages a model has been trained on. — supported_languages

text: Character vector ISO-639-1 codes.

what: Train the learned selection model from human review labels. — train_selection_model
title: Train the learned selection model from human review labels. — train_selection_model
text: Train learned selection model human review labels.

title: Train the learned selection model from human review labels. — train_selection_model
code: train_selection_model(   records,   reviews,   topics,   labels,   learner = selection_learner_logistic(),   eval_source = "random",   include = character(0),   v = 5L,   repeats = 1L,   threshold = 0.5,   seed = NULL )

title: Train the learned selection model from human review labels. — train_selection_model
text: records scored + classified tibble (planscanR::get_assessments(), planscanR::index_cache(), review-app snapshot) carrying selection_features() columns. records also appear reviews keep/drop decision used training. reviews review-decision tibble (app's reviews.csv), document_id, country, decision, source, reviewed_at. topics, labels topic classifier-label vectors naming feature columns (required); see selection_feature_names(). Stored returned model predict_selection() rebuilds feature frame. learner selection_learner. Defaults selection_learner_logistic(). eval_source Restrict labels review source (default "random" — unbiased sample). NULL uses every keep/drop label. include Optional extra feature columns; see selection_features(). v, repeats Cross-validation folds repeats --fold metrics. threshold Default probability cutoff keep decision. seed Optional RNG seed reproducible folds.

title: Train the learned selection model from human review labels. — train_selection_model
text: planscanR_selection_model: fitted workflow plus provenance (learner_name, features, n_train, trained_at, ...), --fold predictions (oof), CV metrics threshold (cv).

title: Train the learned selection model from human review labels. — train_selection_model
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ recs <- index_cache(country = "nl") rev <- read.csv(file.path(cache_dir, "reviews.csv")) m <- train_selection_model(recs, rev) m$cv } # }
